title: jacksonia tenuifolia
intro: Jacksonia tenuifolia, commonly known as the "native pea", is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and flowers of Jacksonia tenuifolia have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and poultices, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
